name: Usersnap Visual Bug Reporting API
what_it_does: Visual feedback and bug reporting platform with REST API — enabling users and QA teams to capture annotated screenshots, attach console logs and session metadata automatically, collect in-product feedback via widgets, route bug reports to Jira/Trello/GitHub, and analyze feedback themes for product teams.
use_cases: Querying user-submitted bug reports and feedback for product/QA triage workflows
Integrating Usersnap feedback with Jira for automatic ticket creation via API
Managing feedback project configuration and user permissions programmatically
Pulling feature request and NPS data for product prioritization analytics
Automating feedback routing to engineering teams based on category and priority
not_for: Error monitoring and crash reporting (use Sentry or Datadog for automated errors)
User session replay without feedback widget (use FullStory or Hotjar for replay)
Customer support ticketing (use Zendesk or Intercom for support workflows)
best_when: A product or QA team wants users and internal testers to submit annotated visual bug reports with automatic technical context (console logs, browser info, network requests) captured alongside screenshots.
avoid_when: You need automated error monitoring, session replay analytics, customer support ticketing, or formal usability testing rather than in-the-moment user feedback.

auth.notes: API key from Usersnap account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. JavaScript SDK for feedback widget embedding. Webhook for feedback submission events. No OAuth2. Jira, Trello, GitHub, Slack native integrations via admin. Zapier for workflow automation.

pricing.notes: Austrian company (Vienna). Founded 2010. Bootstrapped with growth funding. Affordable pricing for teams of all sizes. Visual feedback differentiator — screenshot annotation directly in browser without screen recording. Console log and network request auto-capture. Multiple widget types: bug report, feature request, NPS, custom. Jira and GitHub native integrations. Popular with dev teams for QA and beta testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for feedback items, projects, users, and configurations. Bearer token auth. Good API documentation. JavaScript SDK required for in-app widget. API provides full feedback data including annotations, screenshots (as URLs), and technical metadata. Webhook for new feedback events. Integration configurations in admin.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Feedback reads fully idempotent. Project reads idempotent. User reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: JavaScript SDK required for in-browser feedback widget
Screenshots stored as URLs — implement time-limited access or re-download promptly
Console log capture includes JavaScript errors — may expose sensitive app data
GDPR: user-submitted feedback is PII — implement retention policies
Widget customization requires SDK configuration, not API-driven

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and ISO27001 compliant. EU data residency. Bearer token auth. User feedback may contain sensitive UI/app data in screenshots — review before external sharing. Console log capture may expose tokens or PII — configure selective capture. Austrian company with strong GDPR-first design.
